prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph with motion. London commuter train, window seat area. A Black British woman's right hand holds an iPhone, thumb mid-swipe pushing a shoe card to the left — the card is tilted and partially off-screen, capturing the decisive rejection moment with slight motion blur on the finger and card. Through the window behind, London suburbs flow past in beautiful motion blur — streaks of terraced rooftops, green hedges, and grey sky blending. Morning diffused light enters through the moving window. The phone screen shows a shoe product (a hiking boot with colorful accents — the 'wrong' one). Color grading: neutral with the motion blur creating gentle color streaks through the window. Film-like desaturation. The card on screen has visible detail. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. Phone and swiping hand are center-frame. The flowing window scenery creates dynamic background. The tilted card conveys rejection energy. Camera: 50mm lens, f/2.5, ISO 400. Film grain. The finger has intentional motion blur from the swipe. Window glass reflections catch subtle spectral refraction. The mood is 'this is wrong' — the instant of recognizing a constraint the AI couldn't translate. A quiet, decisive rejection.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic skin, studio lighting, stationary train, dark, face visible, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration
